meta.title: Cybersecurity Framework Profile for Artificial Intelligence

structure.philosophy: Integrates with CSF 2.0. Focuses on outcomes rather than new compliance checklists.

pillars.name: Securing AI Systems
pillars.intent: Integrate AI into the environment without creating new attack surfaces.

controls.title: Infrastructure Hardening
controls.desc: Ensure underlying GPU/TPU clusters and orchestration layers (K8s) are hardened.

controls.title: Model Integrity
controls.desc: Cryptographically sign models to prevent supply chain poisoning (Pickle/Serialization attacks).

controls.title: Data Sanitation
controls.desc: Validate input pipelines to prevent poisoning and injection attacks.

pillars.name: AI Enabled Cyber Defense
pillars.intent: Use AI to enhance threat detection, automation, and defensive operations.

controls.title: Adaptive Threat Detection
controls.desc: Utilize ML models to detect subtle anomalies in network traffic and user behavior.

controls.title: Automated Remediation
controls.desc: Deploy autonomous agents (like Khepra Sonar) to self-heal configuration drift.

pillars.name: Thwarting AI Enabled Cyberattacks
pillars.intent: Prepare for next-gen threats driven by LLMs, deepfakes, and automated exploitation.

controls.title: Anti-Reconnaissance
controls.desc: Detect and disrupt automated AI scrapers and vulnerability scanners.

controls.title: Adversarial Robustness
controls.desc: Defend against prompt injection, jailbreaking, and model inversion attacks.

controls.title: Synthetic Content Detection
controls.desc: Implement mechanisms to identify deepfakes and AI-generated social engineering.
